[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2011-09-02
[TITLE]: Explosion, 3-alarm fire at McDonald’s restaurant in Encinitas – San Diego Union-Tribune
[METADATA/INTRO]: ENCINITASENCINITAS — A three-alarm blaze reportedly caused by a natural-gas explosion destroyed a McDonald’s restaurant Thursday night on Encinitas Boulevard near El Camino Real, a fire official said. Employees called 911 to report the explosion about 8:25 p.m., Encinitas Fire...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Firefighters were able to keep the blaze from spreading to a strip of small businesses adjacent to the McDonald’s Play Place, the restaurant’s play area for children. The nearest business, Miracle-Ear Optometry, had smoke damage, Scott said. 
 Ann Schulz, who said she has owned the McDonald’s for 33 years, watched firefighters from the parking lot. “I’m just so thankful that no one was hurt,” she said. 
 Several McDonald’s employees huddled nearby, but did not want to talk to the media. Scott said the employees did a “great job” of getting everyone out of the restaurant safely. 
 No firefighters were injured. One employee suffered a minor cut to a finger, but declined medical attention at the scene, Scott said.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2021-06-17
[TITLE]: Fire engulfs, destroys Downtown Alton McDonald’s
[METADATA/INTRO]: Watch full coverage of the McDonalds fire A fire ripped through the Downtown McDonald’s early Thursday leaving the restaurant a total loss, Alton Fire Chief Jesse Jemison said....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Watch full coverage of the McDonalds fire 
 A fire ripped through the Downtown McDonald’s early Thursday leaving the restaurant a total loss, Alton Fire Chief Jesse Jemison said. 
 Article continues below this ad 
 The Alton Fire Department arrived at approximately 5:33 a.m. Passersby called 911, reporting smoke coming from the roof of the building at 717 E. Broadway before 5:30 a.m. 
 After an interior attack of the structure fire, which apparently started in or near a HVAC unit on the roof, it progressed quickly. Firefighters from Alton, Godfrey, East Alton and Florissant, Missouri, battled the blaze as the HVAC unit collapsed partially through the roof making the structure too dangerous to enter. All firefighters were pulled from inside the building at that time, setting up fort for an exterior attack, only as the fire spread through the attic towards the front of the building.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2023-11-21
[TITLE]: Monifieth McDonald's fire: Huge blaze erupts at 24-hour restaurant as emergency crews race to scene
[METADATA/INTRO]: FIRE crews are battling a huge blaze at a Scots McDonald’s restaurant. Emergency services were scrambled to the branch at Ethiebeaton Park in Monifieth, Angus, after the alarm was raised around 1:15am....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: FIRE crews are battling a huge blaze at a Scots McDonald’s restaurant. 
 Emergency services were scrambled to the branch at Ethiebeaton Park in Monifieth, Angus, after the alarm was raised around 1:15am. 
 Dramatic images from the scene show the building engulfed in flames as firefighters work to extinguish the inferno. 
 A large plume of thick smoke could be seen billowing into the sky over the town. 
 The cause of the blaze is currently unknown at this stage. 
 All staff at the 24-hour restaurant who were working through the night escaped unharmed. 
 Read more Scottish news 
 A McDonald’s spokesperson said: “Following a serious fire onsite, our Arbroath Road restaurant is currently closed.
